bead.title: test-scrub: gate chaos/property/perf-budget suites behind testing.Short() so per-bead drains use go test -short
bead.description:
PROBLEM/ROOT CAUSE: Only 21 test files honor -short; the ~90s+ internal/bead/{chaos_test.go,chaos2_test.go,property_test.go,store_perf_test.go} and several agent/graphql integration files ignore it, so `go test -short ./...` doesn't speed up the heavy packages. Per-bead drains run the full chaos/property/perf suites every time.

PROPOSED FIX: Add `if testing.Short() { t.Skip("full-run only") }` to the chaos, property, and store-perf tests in internal/bead (and the not-short-honoring integration tests in agent/graphql). Switch the per-bead worker drain test-gate to `go test -short` (full run stays in CI, which does NOT pass -short). Keep the backend-matrix (forEachChaosBackend, chaos_test.go:61) coverage — it's gated (skipped on fast path), not removed.

NON-SCOPE: do NOT delete any chaos/property/backend-matrix coverage; CI must still run them.
bead.acceptance:
1. cd cli && go test -short ./internal/bead/... skips the chaos/property/perf tests and finishes <10s; the non-skipped bead unit tests still run.
2. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... (no -short) still runs ALL chaos/property/perf tests — full CI coverage preserved.
3. TestChaos_*, TestProperty_*, TestPerformance_BeadStoreConcurrentClaimReadyHeartbeatUnderBudget report --- SKIP under -short and --- PASS without it.
4. The per-bead/worker drain test-gate invocation uses -short.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
